evidence_rea: If you have left comments on the Website
an exported file of the personal data we hold about you
evidence_reg: the personal data concerning him or her, which he or she has provided to a controller
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA limits the right to cases where the user 'have left comments on the Website' and to data 'we hold about you', while the REG describes receiving data 'which he or she has provided to a controller'; this changes the data scope/trigger rather than merely omitting details.

evidence_rea: If you have left comments on the Website
the personal data we hold about you
evidence_reg: a copy of the personal data undergoing processing
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA restricts the request to users who 'have left comments on the Website' and refers to data 'we hold about you', whereas the REG covers 'personal data undergoing processing'; this is a meaning-changing alteration of data scope.

evidence_rea: If you have left comments on the Website
an exported file of the personal data we hold about you
evidence_reg: the personal data concerning him or her, which he or she has provided to a controller
(a) the processing is based on consent ... or on a contract
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA makes the entitlement depend on having 'left comments on the Website' and refers to data 'we hold about you', while the REG ties portability to data 'provided to a controller' under specified legal bases; this alters the applicable data scope/trigger.
